title: DPIA Methodology
description: Data Protection Impact Assessment methodology aligned with Malaysia PDPA 2010 and international best practices

legalBasis.description: While PDPA 2010 does not explicitly mandate DPIAs, Sections 6 (General Principle), 9 (Security Principle), 129 (Cross-border transfers), and 129B (Data Protection Officer) collectively require organizations to assess and mitigate data protection risks

phases.name: Screening
phases.description: Determine whether a DPIA is required based on processing characteristics
steps: Identify the data processing activity and its purpose
Determine categories of personal data involved (general vs sensitive under s4)
Assess scale of processing (number of data subjects, data volume, geographic scope)
Check against DPIA threshold criteria
Document screening decision with justification

phases.name: Description
phases.description: Document the nature, scope, context, and purpose of the processing
steps: Map complete data flow from collection to deletion
Identify all data processors and third parties involved (s4 definitions)
Document lawful basis for processing under PDPA principles
Describe technical infrastructure and security measures
Identify cross-border data transfers if any (s129)

phases.name: Necessity & Proportionality
phases.description: Assess whether the processing is necessary and proportionate to its purpose
steps: Verify processing is necessary for stated purpose (General Principle, s6)
Confirm data collected is adequate, relevant, and not excessive (s6(1))
Assess data retention periods against purpose limitation
Evaluate whether purpose can be achieved with less personal data
Review consent mechanisms and data subject notice (s7)

phases.name: Risk Identification
phases.description: Identify risks to data subjects' rights and interests
steps: Identify threats to confidentiality (unauthorized access/disclosure)
Identify threats to integrity (unauthorized modification/corruption)
Identify threats to availability (loss/destruction of data)
Assess risks of function creep (processing beyond original purpose)
Evaluate discrimination or bias risks from processing outcomes
Consider vulnerable data subjects (children, employees, patients)

phases.name: Risk Assessment
phases.description: Evaluate likelihood and severity of identified risks
steps: Rate likelihood of each risk (1-5 scale)
Rate impact/severity on data subjects (1-5 scale)
Calculate risk score (likelihood × impact)
Classify risks using organizational risk matrix
Prioritize risks requiring mitigation

phases.name: Mitigation
phases.description: Identify and implement measures to mitigate identified risks
steps: Design technical controls (encryption, access control, pseudonymization)
Design organizational controls (policies, training, procedures)
Implement data minimization measures
Establish breach notification procedures (s12 Notice Principle alignment)
Define data retention and deletion schedules
Plan for data subject rights handling (access, correction, deletion under s30-s42)

phases.name: Consultation
phases.description: Consult with stakeholders and the Data Protection Officer
steps: Present DPIA findings to Data Protection Officer (s129B)
Consult with affected data subjects or their representatives where feasible
Engage information security team for technical review
Seek legal counsel on compliance gaps identified
Consider consulting JPDP (Commissioner) for high-risk processing

phases.name: Decision & Review
phases.description: Make go/no-go decision and establish ongoing review
steps: Document final risk assessment with residual risks
Obtain senior management approval for residual risk acceptance
Define review triggers (material change, incident, 12-month cycle)
Integrate DPIA into project governance lifecycle
Archive DPIA as compliance evidence

reviewTriggers: Material change to processing purpose or scope
New categories of personal data collected
New technology or processing method introduced
Data breach or security incident related to the processing
Regulatory guidance or enforcement action by JPDP
Change in data processor or cross-border transfer arrangements
